2026-04-01T02:50:19.4641321Z 
2026-04-01T02:50:22.9997307Z Posted 2 review comments on PR #73:
2026-04-01T02:50:22.9997865Z 
2026-04-01T02:50:23.7798364Z 1. **`llm_config_service.dart` line 192** — The `DateTime.now().millisecondsSinceEpoch` fallback for `slotId` is non-deterministic; the same backend record would get a different `slotId` on every parse call. Suggested using a deterministic fallback or throwing an assertion.
2026-04-01T02:50:23.7799844Z 
2026-04-01T02:50:24.4082899Z 2. **`docs/model_settings_plan.md` line 1** — The file is an internal planning artifact rather than durable documentation; should be removed before merging.
2026-04-01T02:50:24.4083570Z 
2026-04-01T02:50:25.5756307Z No blockers found. The core changes (multi-slot config list, `save()` returning the persisted object, hardened `Map` casting, `gemini-flash-latest` default, `mounted` guards) all look correct.

2026-04-01T02:50:29.2632076Z Agent: If `fetchConfigs()` is called multiple times, the same backend record might get different slotIds, which could cause correctness issues. 2026-04-01T02:50:29.2653256Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T02:50:29.2656703Z Agent: Now I'm weighing which issues are worth flagging: the DateTime fallback for slotId is a real correctness concern if the backend ever returns missing IDs, though that shouldn't happen in practice; the config mutation outside setState is a minor Flutter style issue since setState is called immediately after; and there's a planning document worth mentioning. I'm tracing through what happens when the API response isn't a Map—the config with the user's API key gets returned and stored in memory, then cleared from the UI but still present in the `_configs` list. This is a potential security issue if that in-memory object gets logged or serialized, though it's transient and would be replaced on the next fetch. The real question is what the API should actually return on a successful save.

When the workflow is configured with `target: \"*\"`, you must specify `pull_request_number` to indicate which PR to target. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 10 review comment(s) can be created. Comments will be on the RIGHT side of the diff.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Review comment content in Markdown. Provide specific, actionable feedback about the code at this location.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"line":{"description":"Line number for the comment. For single-line comments, this is the target line. For multi-line comments, this is the ending line.","type":["number","string"]},"path":{"description":"File path relative to the repository root (e.g., 'src/auth/login.js'). Must be a file that was changed in the PR.","type":"string"},"pull_request_number":{"description":"Pull request number to add the review comment to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 876 in github.com/owner/repo/pull/876). If omitted, adds the comment to the PR that triggered this workflow. Required when the workflow target is '*' (any PR) — omitting it will cause the comment to fail.","type":["number","string"]},"repo":{"description":"Target repository in 'owner/repo' format. 2026-04-01T02:50:29.5156430Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-04-01T02:50:29.5156706Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-04-01T02:50:29.5156859Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-01T02:50:29.5157072Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-04-01T02:50:29.5157441Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
